original_abstract: This chapter examines the critical factors influencing the adoption and implementation of artificial intelligence (AI) across the African continent. The analysis begins by defining "responsible AI," contextualizing the term specifically for technologies developed and deployed within African socio-economic frameworks. The study identifies and explores significant obstacles to progress, including systemic gaps in digital literacy, a shortage of local specialized talent, and restrictive governmental barriers. Furthermore, the chapter provides a comprehensive evaluation of the burgeoning African AI research and startup ecosystems, highlighting key organizations and emerging trends of concern. Concluding with a forward-looking perspective, the chapter envisions a sustainable model for responsible AI in Africa and offers a series of actionable recommendations to accelerate progress toward this objective.
humanized_abstract: Chapter 6: Africa Chapter 6: Africa explores the factors that will determine the success of the adoption and implementation of AI in Africa. This chapter begins by examining the concept of “responsible AI” and as it applies to AI developed and deployed in an African context. It then delves into the barriers it encountered including digital literacy gaps, lack of relevant local skills, and government restrictions. The chapter provides an in-depth assessment of the current state of African AI research and startup innovation, including notable initiatives and concerns. Finally, it concludes with a vision for a sustainable future of responsible AI in Africa and provides concrete recommendations to achieve it.
